description: Overview of the fundamental concepts of training theory: adaptation, supercompensation, periodisation, training load, and training planning.

questions.text: What is adaptation in the context of training?
questions.explanation: Adaptation is the body's adjustment to training load: organs and systems are strengthened in response to repeated stimuli.

options.text: The body's adjustment to training load
The name of the training plan

questions.text: Supercompensation is the phenomenon of performance improvement following training.
questions.explanation: True. During supercompensation, the body rises to a higher level after recovery than it was before the training stimulus.

questions.text: What is the purpose of periodisation in training planning?
questions.explanation: Periodisation is the systematic, planned distribution of training work over time so that the athlete reaches peak form at the right moment.

options.text: Performing the same intensity every day
The planned distribution of training work over time

questions.text: Which is one of the main components of training load?
questions.explanation: The main components of training load are intensity, volume, density, and duration. Intensity indicates the magnitude of the load.

questions.text: What is the main goal of training planning?
questions.explanation: The goal of training planning is to achieve regular, systematic development through the conscious distribution of load and rest.

options.text: To complete as many sessions as possible
To shorten sessions as much as possible

questions.text: Feedback is required to control training.
questions.explanation: True. Feedback (e.g., training diaries, tests) enables the plan to be modified based on results achieved.

questions.text: What is the role of training principles?
questions.explanation: Training principles (e.g., progressiveness, specificity, variety) provide the scientific foundation for systematic and effective training.

options.text: They prescribe arbitrary execution of training
To ensure training effectiveness and safety

questions.text: A rest period is required for supercompensation to occur.
questions.explanation: True. Adaptation takes place during rest; without recovery, supercompensation cannot develop.
A training method is a systematic procedure applied to achieve a training goal, defining how the load is organised.

options.text: The name of the training venue
A systematic procedure applied to achieve a training goal

questions.text: Sustained performance improvement is not possible without adaptation.
questions.explanation: True. Training only leads to development if the body adapts to the stimulus — adaptation is the biological basis of improvement.
